_description: Generate ending frame from Frame A. Stage 4. Attach ONLY Frame A as reference (ONE image). Product is already in Frame A.
_usage: Single reference image: Frame A output. Fill changes per script. Everything not listed in changes stays IDENTICAL.

image_1.instruction: This is Frame A. It defines EVERYTHING: person, product, environment, lighting, camera. Generate Frame B where ONLY the specified changes occur. Everything else is pixel-identical

frame_b_changes.timing: [FILL: X seconds after Frame A. Typically 2-3 seconds]
frame_b_changes.script_beat: [FILL: what happens at this moment per script. e.g. 'She leans in to verify what she's seeing']
frame_b_changes.expression: [FILL: specific changes FROM Frame A expression. Not the absolute expression, the DELTA. e.g. 'Eyes narrow from wide to focused, brow furrows, mouth closes from parted']
frame_b_changes.head_position: [FILL: degrees of change. e.g. 'Tilt reduces from 3° to 1°, head moves forward 5cm, chin juts slightly']
frame_b_changes.distance_to_camera: [FILL: 'CLOSER by Xcm' or 'SAME' or 'FURTHER by Xcm'. If closer, face appears larger in frame and distortion increases]
frame_b_changes.body: [FILL: e.g. 'Lean forward from waist, shoulders hunch slightly' or 'SAME']
frame_b_changes.free_hand: [FILL: if hand position changes, specify. Otherwise 'IDENTICAL to Frame A']
frame_b_changes.product_in_hand: IDENTICAL to Frame A. Same grip, same angle. Scale adjusts proportionally with distance change

product_scale_consistency.rule: Product must scale PROPORTIONALLY with face size change between frames
product_scale_consistency.if_closer: Face is LARGER in Frame B → product must ALSO be larger (same hand, same distance to camera as face, they scale together)
product_scale_consistency.if_same_distance: Face same size → product same size
product_scale_consistency.if_further: Face is SMALLER → product also smaller
product_scale_consistency.physics: Product is in the same hand at the same relative distance from camera as the face. Model CANNOT enlarge face without enlarging product
product_scale_consistency.common_failure: Model shrinks product when face gets larger. This is physically impossible. Regenerate if this occurs

MUST_STAY_IDENTICAL: Character identity (face, skin, moles, scars, all signature details)
Clothing (same outfit, same wrinkles, same collar position)
Product (same item, same grip, PROPORTIONAL scale)
Environment (same background, same objects, nothing moved)
Camera position (phone hasn't moved, framing anchor same)
allowed_micro_changes: Lighting intensity on face (if distance changed, physically accurate)
Screen glow on chin (brighter if closer to phone)
Hair strand position (1-2 strands shift from head movement)
negative_prompt: product smaller than Frame A (if subject is closer)
[FILL: expression-specific negatives per script, e.g. 'smiling' if script says scrutinizing]
